User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual who can log into the application, associated with a specific role and optionally linked to a library member profile. This entity links to an external authentication system via email.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: User's email address, serving as a unique identifier for their application account and linking to the external authentication system.

role.description: The role of the user within the application (e.g., 'Admin', 'Officer', 'Teacher', 'Student').

libraryMemberId.description: Reference to the LibraryMember entity if the user's role is 'Teacher' or 'Student'. (Relationship: User 1:1 LibraryMember)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

LibraryMember.title: LibraryMember
LibraryMember.description: Represents an individual registered as a library member, either a student or a teacher, with their personal and contact details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LibraryMember entity.

memberType.description: Specifies the type of library member (e.g., 'Student', 'Teacher').

uniqueIdNumber.description: The unique identification number for the member (NIP for teachers, NIS for students). This number is unique per member type.

fullName.description: The full name of the library member.

contactNumber.description: The contact phone number of the library member.

address.description: The residential address of the library member.

classOrSubject.description: For students, their class; for teachers, their subject.

registrationDate.description: Date when the member was registered in the library system.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity associated with this library member, if they have an application account. (Relationship: LibraryMember 1:1 User)

BookCategory.title: BookCategory
BookCategory.description: Defines categories used to classify books in the library for organization and searchability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the book category (e.g., 'Fiksi', 'Sejarah', 'Sains').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

ShelfLocation.title: ShelfLocation
ShelfLocation.description: Represents the physical location or shelf where books are stored in the library for efficient retrieval.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShelfLocation entity.

name.description: The name or code of the shelf location (e.g., 'Rak A1', 'Lantai 2 - Bagian Fiksi').

description.description: A description of the shelf location.

Book.title: Book
Book.description: Represents a single book title available in the library, including its bibliographic details, stock information, and physical location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Book entity, often an auto-generated book code.

title.description: The full title of the book.

publicationYear.description: The year the book was published.

isbn.description: The International Standard Book Number, unique for each book edition.

totalStock.description: The total number of physical copies of this book owned by the library.

availableStock.description: The number of copies of this book currently available for borrowing.

coverImageUrl.description: URL to the book's cover image.

description.description: A summary or abstract of the book's content, potentially enhanced by AI.

bookCategoryId.description: Reference to the BookCategory this book belongs to. (Relationship: Book 1:N BookCategory)

shelfLocationId.description: Reference to the ShelfLocation where this book is primarily stored. (Relationship: Book 1:N ShelfLocation)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the book record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the book record was last updated.

BorrowingTransaction.title: BorrowingTransaction
BorrowingTransaction.description: Records a single instance of a book being borrowed by a library member, including details about dates, status, and fines.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BorrowingTransaction entity.

borrowDate.description: The date and time when the book was borrowed.

dueDate.description: The scheduled date and time when the book is due for return.

returnDate.description: The actual date and time when the book was returned. Null if not yet returned.

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'Borrowed', 'Returned', 'Overdue').

fineAmount.description: The amount of fine incurred for overdue returns, if any.

isFinePaid.description: Indicates whether the incurred fine has been paid by the member.

libraryMemberId.description: Reference to the LibraryMember who borrowed the book. (Relationship: LibraryMember 1:N BorrowingTransaction)

bookId.description: Reference to the Book that was borrowed. (Relationship: Book 1:N BorrowingTransaction)

processedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (admin or officer) who processed this borrowing transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N BorrowingTransaction)

ActivityLog.title: ActivityLog
ActivityLog.description: Records significant actions performed by users within the application for auditing, tracking, and compliance purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActivityLog entry.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed the action. (Relationship: User 1:N ActivityLog)

actionType.description: A brief code or name for the type of action performed (e.g., 'BOOK_ADD', 'MEMBER_UPDATE', 'TRANSACTION_BORROW').

description.description: A detailed description of the action and its context.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the action occurred.

OverdueNotification.title: OverdueNotification
OverdueNotification.description: Records details of notifications sent to library members regarding overdue books, facilitating follow-up and management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OverdueNotification entity.

borrowingTransactionId.description: Reference to the BorrowingTransaction for which the notification was sent. (Relationship: BorrowingTransaction 1:N OverdueNotification)

notificationDate.description: The date and time when the notification was sent.

method.description: The method used to send the notification (e.g., 'Email', 'In-App Message', 'SMS').

status.description: The delivery status of the notification (e.g., 'Sent', 'Failed', 'Read').

definition.description: Stores user profiles, including email, role, and a reference to their library member profile. The {userId} wildcard must correspond to the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'role' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores details of library members (students/teachers). Includes denormalized 'userId' to link to an application user, enabling authorization independence for self-access.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the library member.

definition.description: Master data collection for various book categories. Publicly readable, staff-manageable.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the book category.

definition.description: Master data collection for physical shelf locations in the library. Publicly readable, staff-manageable.

params.name: locationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shelf location.

definition.description: Stores detailed information about each book title, including stock levels and references to category and shelf location. All authenticated users can read, staff can manage.

params.name: bookId
params.description: The unique identifier of the book.

definition.description: Records individual book borrowing events. Includes denormalized 'memberUserId' for direct member access and 'processedByUserId' for staff access, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the borrowing transaction.

definition.description: Records significant user actions for auditing purposes. Includes the 'userId' of the actor for direct authorization checks. All authenticated users can write, Admins can read.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier of the activity log entry.

definition.description: Tracks notifications sent for overdue books. Includes denormalized 'memberUserId' from the associated borrowing transaction to enable direct member access and authorization independence.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the overdue notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support a modern school library information system, prioritizing secure, scalable, and debuggable access control. The core principle of Authorization Independence is achieved through strategic denormalization, specifically by embedding relevant user IDs directly into documents where access might depend on user identity.

For example, the 'User' collection (`/users/{userId}`) is the central point for authentication and role management. Each user document stores the `role` (Admin, Officer, Teacher, Student) directly, allowing security rules to instantly determine a user's permissions via `request.auth.uid` without costly `get()` operations. The `userId` in this path corresponds directly to the Firebase Authentication UID.

To ensure QAPs (Query-Acceptance Policies) and maintain Authorization Independence for user-specific data like library member profiles, borrowing transactions, and overdue notifications, the following denormalization strategies are applied:

1.  **LibraryMember Data:** The `/library_members/{memberId}` collection stores individual library member details. For members who also have an application account, their `userId` (from the `/users` collection) is explicitly included in their `LibraryMember` document. This allows a logged-in user to directly query or retrieve their own `LibraryMember` profile using a `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clause, and security rules can grant access based on this field without performing a `get()` on the `User` collection.

2.  **BorrowingTransaction Data:** The `/borrowing_transactions/{transactionId}` collection includes two crucial denormalized fields: `memberUserId` (the `userId` of the `User` associated with the borrowing `LibraryMember`) and `processedByUserId` (the `userId` of the staff `User` who processed the transaction). This design allows security rules to evaluate access for both the borrowing member (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.memberUserId`) and the processing staff (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.processedByUserId`) atomically. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to other collections (like 'LibraryMember' or 'User') to determine transaction ownership or processing authority.

3.  **OverdueNotification Data:** Similarly, the `/overdue_notifications/{notificationId}` collection denormalizes the `memberUserId` from the associated `BorrowingTransaction`. This enables a member to easily query for and access notifications directly related to their own overdue books using `where('memberUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)`, with security rules enforcing this ownership without additional `get()` operations.

Collections like `BookCategory`, `ShelfLocation`, and `Book` are designed as top-level collections with a homogeneous security posture (e.g., generally readable by all authenticated users, writable by staff), making their security rules straightforward and avoiding `get()` dependencies. `ActivityLog` entries include the `userId` of the actor, facilitating auditing without external lookups. This structure ensures that common access patterns are simple to secure and performant for QAPs, as relevant authorization fields are always present within the document being accessed or easily queryable.
